verses.text: It was when the judges were judging, there was a famine in the land, and a went from Baithleem in Iouda to sojourn in countryside of Moab, he and his wife and his s

The name of the man was Abimelech, and name of his wife, Noemin, and the name of two sons, Maalon and Chelaion, Ephrathites f Baithleem in Iouda. They went into the cou side of Moab and remained there.
And melech, the husband of Noemin, died, and was left, she and her two sons.

They took themselves Moabite wives; the name of the was Orpha, and the name of the second, Ro And they lived there about ten years, 5and Maalon and Chelaion also died, and the wo was left, apart from her husband and apart f her two sons.
And she set out, she and her two daugh in-law, and they came back from the country of Moab, for they had heard in the countrysid Moab that the Lord had alooked upona his pe givingb them bread.

And she went out from place, there where she had been, and her daughters-in-law with her, and they were goin their way to return to the land of Iouda, 8 Noemin said to her daughters-in-law, “Go no back, each to her mother’s house. May the Lor mercy with you, as you have done with the and with me.
May the Lord give to you, and you find rest, each in the house of her husba And she kissed them, and they lifted up their v and wept.
They said to her, “We are retur with you to your people.”

And Noemin “Turn back now, my daughters! Now why are going with me? Do I still have sons in my wo and will they become husbands for you? 12 back now, my daughters, for I am too old for a band to have me. For if I said, ‘I have confid that a man will marry me and I will bear s 13you would not wait for them until they ma would you? Or would you, on their account frain from getting married? Don’t, my daugh for it has been more bitter for me than for you cause the hand of the Lord has gone forth ag me.”
And they lifted up their voice and wept ther. Orpha kissed her mother-in-law and retur to her people, but Routh followed her.
And Noemin said to Routh, “See, your ter-in-law has turned back to her people and to gods; now turn back, you too, after your siste law.”
But Routh said,

“cDo not oppose me, to make me aband you or turn backc from after you. For wherever you go, I will go, and wherever you lodge, I will lodge; your people shall be my people, and your God my God. And wherever you die, I will die— there will I be buried. Thus and so may the Lord do to me, aOr visited bOr to give cMay it not befall me to aban fI.e. Listen, daughter uth 1–2 241 nd and thus and so may he add— an the ns. the his om trybishe for ne th. oth an om erside of ple, the two on nd, go do ead ay d.” ice ing aid, you b, urn usnce ns,’ ure, reers, beinst fured sisher -inn and the Sufficient One has maltreated me?”
And Noemin returned, and Routh the Moabite, her daughter-in-law, returning from the countryside of Moab. Now they came to Baithleem at the beginning of the barley harvest.
And with regard to Noemin there was a man, an acquaintance of her husband, and the man was powerful, of the kin of Abimelech, and his name was Boos.
And Routh the Moabite said to Noemin, “Let me go now to the field and gather among the ears of grain, behind anyone in whose eyes I may find favor.” And she said to her, “Go, daughter.”
And she went and gleaned in the field behind the reapers. And by happenstance she happened upon the part of the field belonging to Boos, who was of the kin of Abimelech.
And there, from Baithleem, came Boos! He said to the reapers, “The Lord be with you.” They said to him, “The Lord bless you.”
And Boos said to his servant who was in charge of the reapers, “To whom does this young woman belong?”
The servant who was in charge of the reapers answered and said, “She is the Moabite lass, the one who came back with Noemin from the countryside of Moab.
She said, ‘Let me glean now and gather among the sheaves behind the reapers.’ And she came, and she stood from morning until evening. She did not rest a bit in the field.”
And Boos said to Routh, “fDid you not hear, daughterf? Do not go to glean in another field, and you should not go from this one. Stick close to my girls here.

Keepd your eyes on the field, wherever they are reaping, and walk behind them. See, I have commanded the young men not to touch you. If you want anything to drink, go to the vessels, and drink from what the young men draw.”
And she fell upon her face and did obeisance on the ground and said to him, “Why is it that I have found favor in your eyes, that you should take notice of me? And I am a foreigner!”

And Boos answered and said to her, “All that you have done together with your mother-in-law since the death of your husband has been fully told me, and how you on you or to turn back = Ra dLacking in Gk eI.e. the women d for death alone shall part me from you!”
Then when Noemin saw that she was determined to go with her, she ceased speaking to her further.
So the two of them went on until they came to Baithleem. And the whole city was abuzz because of them, and theye said, “Is this Noemin?”
She said to them,

“Call me no longer Noemin; call me Bitter, for the Sufficient One was greatly embittered against me. I went away full, and the Lord has brought me back empty. Why do you call me Noemin when the Lord has abased me 242 rou left your father and your mother and your native land and came to a people that you did not know yesterday or the day before. May the Lord repay you for your work, and may your recompense be full from the Lord, God of Israel, to whom you came, to put your trust under his wings!” Then she said, “May I find favor in your eyes, sir, because you have comforted me and because you have spoken to the heart of your slave, and see, I shall be like one of your maidservants.” And Boos said to her, “It is already time to eat. Come here, and eat some of the bread, and dip your morsel in the wine vinegar.” And Routh sat by the side of the reapers, and Boos heaped up barley groats for her. She ate and was satisfied and had some left over. And she got up to glean, and Boos commanded his young men, saying, “Let her glean even among the standing sheaves, and do not dishonor her. By all means carry for her, and even throw aside something for her from the bundles, and leave it, and she will glean, and do not rebuke her.” And she gleaned in the field until evening and beat out what she had gleaned, and it was about an oiphia of barley. She picked it up and came into the city, and her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned. And taking it out, Routh gave her what she had left over after she had been satisfied. Her mother-in-law said to her, “Where did you glean today? And where did you work? Blessed be the man who took notice of you.” And Routh told her mother-in-law where she worked and said, “The name of the man with whom I worked today is Boos.” And Noemin said to her daughter-in-law, “He is blessed by the Lord, because he has not forsaken his compassion for the living and for the dead.” And Noemin said to her, “The man is near to us; he is one of our next-ofkin.” And Routh said to her mother-in-law, “He even said to me, ‘Stick with my servantsb until they have finished all the harvest that belongs to me.’ ”
Noemin said to Routh, her daughter-in-law, “It is good, daughter, that you go with his girls, and theyc will not confront you in another field.”
And Routh stayed close to the girls of Boos, gleaningd until he completed the reaping of the barley and the wheat, and she lived with her mother-in-law.
But Noemin her mother-in-law said to her, “Daughter, shall I not seek rest for you so that it may be well with you?
Now, is not Boos our acquaintance, whose girls you were with? Look, he is winnowing the threshing floor of the barleye this very night.
Now bathe, and apply oil, and put your clothing on yourself, and go up onto the threshing floor. Do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished drinking and eating.

And it shall be, when he lies down, that you shall observe the place there where he is lying, and you shall go aHeb 1 ephah = 22 liters bOr young men cI.e. people d f I.e. part of the cloak gOr she hOr fall short iPossibly f it to your attention h 2–4 and uncover the place at his feet and lie down, and he will tell you what you shall do.”
Then Routh said to her, “Everything that you say I will do.”
And she went down to the threshing floor and did according to everything that her motherin-law had commanded her.
And Boos ate, and his heart was content, and he went to lie down on part of the heap of grain. Then she came stealthily and uncovered the place at his feet.
Now it was at midnight, and the man was startled and troubled, and there, lying at his feet, was a woman!
So he said, “Who are you?” Then she answered, “I am Routh, your slave; spread your wingf over your bondwoman, for you are a next-of-kin.”
Boos said, “May you be blessed by the Lord God, daughter, for your last instance of compassion is better than the first, that you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich.

And now, daughter, do not be afraid. Everything that you say I will do for you, for the whole clan of my people knows that you are a strong woman 12and that I am, truly, a near kinsman, yet there is even a nearer kinsman than I.

Spend the night, and it shall be in the morning, if he will act as next-of-kin for you, good; let him do it. But if he is not willing to act as nextof-kin for you, the Lord lives, I myself will act as next-of-kin for you. Lie down until morning.”
Andshelayathisfeetuntilmorningbutgot up before a man could recognize his neighbor. And Boos said, “It must not be known that a woman came to the threshing floor.”
And he said to her, “Bring the apron that you have on.” And she held it, and he measured out six measures of barley and put it upon her, and heg went into the city.

And Routh came in to her mother-inlaw, and she said, “Who are you, daughter?” And she told her all that the man had done for her, 17and she said to her, “He gave me these six measures of barley, for he said to me, ‘Do not go in empty-handed to your mother-in-law.’ ”
Then she replied, “Sit, daughter, until you observe how not a word will failh, for certainly the man will not rest until he isettles the matteri today.”

And Boos went up to the gate and sat down there, and right there, passing by, was the nextof-kin of whom Boos had spoken. And Boos said to him, “Come over; sit down here, jHidden One j.” And he went over and sat down.
And Boos took ten men of the elders of the city and said, “Sit

down here,” and they sat down. And Boos said to the next-of-kin, “Concerning the portion of the field that belongs to our brother Abimelech, which has been given to Noemin, who returned from the countryside of Moab,

I said, I will kuncover your eark, saying: Buy it in the presence of those sitting here and in the presence of the elders of my people. If you are acting as next-of-kin, act as next-ofkin, but if you are not acting as next-of-kin, tell me, and I shall know, for there is no one except you to r in order to glean ePossibly barley on the threshing floor lfills his word jI.e. apparently Name Withheld kI.e. bring act as next-of-kin, and I come after you.” S said, “I am the one; I will act as next-of-kin.” 5 Boos said, “The day you acquire the field from hand of Noemin and from Routh the Moabite, wife of the deceased, it is necessary for you t quire her also in order to raise up the name of deceased on his inheritance.”
And the next-of said, “I will not be able to perform the kinsm function for myself without ruining my in tance. Take for yourself my right of inheritance I will not be able to act as next-of-kin.”

And this was the statute in former times i rael concerning the right of inheritance and cerning exchange: to confirm every agreeme man would take off his sandal and give it to neighbor who was acquiring the right of in tance. This was an attestation in Israel.
And next-of-kin said to Boos, “Acquire for yourself right of inheritance,” and he took off his sa and gave it to him.
And Boos said to the el and all the people, “Today you are witnesses t have acquired everything of Abimelech’s an that belongs to Chelaion and Maalon from hand of Noemin.

I have also acquired Routh Moabite, the wife of Maalon, for myself as a to raise up the name of the deceased on his in itance, and the name of the deceased shall no extirpated from his brothers and from the cla his people. Today you are witnesses.”

And all people who were at the gate said, “We area nesses.” And the elders said, “May the Lord aLacking in Gk bPerhaps foster mother outh 4 243 he nd the the acthe kin n’s erifor Isont, a his erithe my dal ers at I all the the ife, erbe of the itake your wife who is coming into your house like Rachel and like Leia, who built up, both of them, the house of Israel and produced might in Ephratha, and there shall be a name in Baithleem.
And, through the offspring that the Lord will give you from this maidservant, may your house be like the house of Phares whom Thamar bore to Iouda.”
And Boos took Routh, and she became a wife to him. And he went in to her, and the Lord granted her conception, and she bore a son.
And the women said to Noemin, “Blessed be the Lord, who did not bring your next-of-kin to an end this day! And may he call your name in Israel!
He shall be to you a restorer of life and shall sustain your gray head. For your daughter-in-law, who loves you, who is better to you than seven sons, has borne him.”
And Noemin took the child and laid him in her bosom and became a nannyb to him.
The women of the neighborhood gave him a name, saying, “A son has been born to Noemin.” They named him Obed. This was the father of Iessai, the father of Dauid.
And these are the generations of Phares: Phares became the father of Hesron, 19then Hesron became the father of Arran, and Arran became the father of Aminadab;
Aminadab became the father of Naasson; Naasson became the father of Salman;
Salman became the father of Boos; Boos became the father of Obed;

Obed became the father of Iessai, and Iessai became the father of Dauid.
